message:
Implement core library entrypoints and logging infrastructure

Establish minimal main.rs and lib.rs to wire up application startup,
environment loading, and structured logging configuration.

main.rs implementation:
- Tokio async main function decorated with #[tokio::main] macro for
  multi-threaded runtime initialization (enables rt-multi-thread
  feature for work-stealing scheduler across all CPU cores)
- Early dotenvy::dotenv().ok() call before any environment variable
  access, following AGENTS.md line 15-26 mandatory pattern (load .env
  at startup, ignore if missing via .ok(), then all downstream code
  can safely use env::var)
- Structured logging setup with tracing-subscriber crate:
  - EnvFilter::try_from_default_env() reads RUST_LOG environment
    variable for dynamic log level control (e.g., RUST_LOG=debug,
    RUST_LOG=coding_agent_search=trace) with fallback to "info"
    level if unset
  - Compact formatting (.compact()) for concise terminal output
    without excessive timestamp/metadata noise during development
  - Disabled target logging (.with_target(false)) to reduce visual
    clutter in CLI output, as module paths are rarely needed for
    user-facing messages in a focused application
- Delegates to coding_agent_search::run() for actual application
  logic, keeping main.rs minimal per Rust conventions (all business
  logic lives in library crate for testability)

lib.rs structure:
- Public module declarations for seven architectural layers: config,
  connectors, indexer, model, search, storage, ui
- Placeholder run() function returning Ok(()) to establish API
  surface for main.rs integration
- Async signature (async fn run()) to support tokio-based concurrent
  operations in future implementation (connector scanning, filesystem
  watching, search queries)

Logging rationale:
Structured logging via tracing (not println! or eprintln!) enables:
- Runtime-configurable verbosity without recompilation (via RUST_LOG)
- Semantic log levels (error/warn/info/debug/trace) for filtering
  signal from noise during development and troubleshooting
- Future integration with tracing spans for async task tracking and
  performance profiling once indexer and search are implemented
- AGENTS.md line 57-60 emphasizes colorful, informative output;
  tracing-subscriber's formatting can be enhanced with ANSI color
  support (via ansi feature flag already enabled in Cargo.toml)

The compact formatter without targets strikes a balance for CLI
applications: structured enough for debugging (timestamps, levels)
but not so verbose as to overwhelm interactive terminal use. For
production deployments requiring machine-readable logs, the JSON
subscriber (enabled via json feature flag) can be swapped in without
code changes.

Empty run() function is intentional scaffolding per implementation
checklist (PLAN § 14.2: core modules, then minimal TUI, then
connectors). This commit establishes the executable structure; logic
follows in subsequent commits as each layer is implemented.

Related: AGENTS.md line 15-26 (dotenvy pattern), line 57-60 (console
output requirements), PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 3.1
(CLI entrypoint), § 3.8 (logging), README.md (environment loading note).
